title: VLM3: Vision Language Models Are Native 3D Learners
summary: VLM3 argues that Vision Language Models are native 3D learners: a large-scale study shows that focal length unification, text-based pixel reference, and data mixture and scaling are all that is needed for effective 3D learning — model architecture changes, large models, heavy augmentations, and complex losses are not necessary conditions. The proposed method is a scalable approach with the simplest design that enables standard VLMs to master diverse 3D tasks. VLM3 advances VLM depth estimation accuracy by a large margin (0.84 → 0.9) and enables tasks such as pixel correspondence, camera pose estimation, and object-level 3D understanding, matching expert vision model accuracy while maintaining standard architectures and text-based training.
